content: Remove obsolete .gitignore entries when tools are deprecated, but preserve entries explicitly required by project coordinators

Archive obsolete feature branches by extracting their unique content to archive/ subdirectories. For streamlit-frontend branches, archived complete frontend code and documentation to archive/streamlit-frontend/ preserving full implementation history.

Code cleanup requires comprehensive plan document listing all files to delete, merge, or modify. Include phased execution with risk mitigation, verification commands, and success criteria. Created docs/plans/CLEANUP_PLAN.md addressing 8 technical debt areas identified in backend analysis.

name: dx-modernization-workflow
description: DX modernization: Create pyproject.toml from requirements.txt for uv migration. Pin deps to exact versions. Fix start.sh paths when frontend is at src/frontend/. Add typecheck script to package.json. Note: builder scope overlap is common - document overlapping files in merge_ready.

content: Code cleanup workflow: 4-phase execution (safe removals → layer consolidation → security hardening → code quality fixes) minimizes risk by ordering changes from low to high impact

Python dependency management: Use pyproject.toml for dependency specification (with ranges) and requirements.txt for pinned versions. This dual approach supports both flexible development (pip install -e .) and reproducible deployments (pip install -r requirements.txt). Pin all versions in requirements.txt using == for exact reproducibility.

Document broken tests before cleanup: When discovering tests with import errors from old architecture (e.g., deleted 'frontend' or 'backend' modules), create BROKEN_TESTS.md documenting each broken test file with status (DELETE or NEEDS_REVIEW) before removing them. Run 'pytest --collect-only' to identify import errors. Total test count and working test count helps assess cleanup impact. Prevents accidental deletion of salvageable tests.
Root directory cleanup: Archive test data (embeddings/, examples/) rather than deleting to preserve historical context. Move utility scripts to scripts/ directory for better organization.

pyproject.toml supersedes pytest.ini: When pyproject.toml has [tool.pytest.ini_options] section, pytest.ini is redundant and should be deleted. Verify pytest config in pyproject.toml is complete before deleting pytest.ini.

name: env-config-consolidation
description: When merging duplicate .env files (.env.example vs .env.template), extract all used env vars by grepping codebase, merge into canonical file, then delete duplicate.

content: Python module shadowing: When both a module file (e.g., src/config.py) and package directory (src/config/) exist with the same name, Python ALWAYS prefers the package. The module file is effectively shadowed and unused. Before deleting shadowed modules, test imports directly with python -c to confirm they're broken/unused. Imports like 'from src.config import config_manager' will look in the package first, not the module file.

name: cherry-pick-cleanup-workflow
description: When a cleanup branch fails merge due to conflicts, extract unique valuable changes (specific git operations) and re-implement them directly on a fresh branch. This avoids complex merge conflicts while preserving cleanup intent. Document what each change accomplishes and verify no dependencies before deletions.

name: test-cleanup-workflow
description: Test cleanup workflow: Run pytest --collect-only to identify broken tests with import errors, delete tests that reference deleted architecture (frontend.components, backend modules), move non-test helpers/docs to tests/helpers/ subdirectory, update import statements in dependent tests, verify with pytest collection showing zero errors

content: Organize test infrastructure: Keep only actual test files in tests/ root, move helper scripts and documentation files to tests/helpers/ subdirectory to prevent pytest from attempting to collect them

Cherry-pick conflict resolution for test files: Prefer combining test classes over choosing one side. When both branches add comprehensive tests to the same file, merge by extracting all test classes, combining imports, and using the more detailed docstring. Result: test_embedding_provider.py went from 263/373 lines to 603 lines (10 test classes total).

Rebase-merge conflict resolution for parallel package manager migrations: When two feature branches migrate different package managers (Python→uv, JavaScript→bun), merge by combining both changes in documentation and scripts. Keep uv for Python commands, bun for JavaScript commands, and remove competing tools (npm/yarn).

name: Worktree quality gates scoping
description: When assigned documentation-only fixes in worktrees, pre-existing test/lint failures in untouched frontend code should be reported but don't block completion. Verify changed files (docs/scripts) are correct; failures in unchanged TypeScript files are outside task scope.

content: Standardizing JavaScript package manager: When migrating to a single JS package manager (bun), remove lockfile artifacts (package-lock.json), update all scripts/docs to use the new tool exclusively, and make it a required dependency rather than optional fallback.
